body.title: CC session name: <hostname>.cc.0 (and .cc.1 for second instance)
body.result: constants._detect_session_name() for N-detection; granny uses hostname.cc.0 default; stop_cc_minions protects .cc.0+.cc.1; autocompact uses $CC_TMUX_SESSION; 9 tests
body.description:
Right now CC_TMUX_SESSION defaults to 'claude-main' (devices/claude/constants.py:24). The web UI and Granny both reference this as 'CC.0' but the tmux session name gives no hint of hostname or instance number — you can only infer when a session started.

Desired: session name of the form <hostname>.cc.0 for the primary instance, <hostname>.cc.1 for a second concurrent instance, etc. This makes multi-host setups and concurrent CC instances visually distinct in tmux ls and the web UI feeds.

**Affected files:** devices/claude/constants.py (update default TMUX_SESSION), devices/granny/daemon.py (update workers_cfg default session key), possibly lab/claudecode/stop_cc_minions.py (update protected-sessions set if pattern changes)

**Design rules:** Keep CC_TMUX_SESSION env var override so existing setups aren't broken. Instance number detection: check tmux for existing <hostname>.cc.N sessions and use next available N.

**Scope boundary:** IN — change default session name format to <hostname>.cc.N; update protected-sessions list; update Granny workers_cfg default. OUT — changing how sessions are dispatched to, renaming existing running sessions.

**Completion criteria:** Default tmux session name is <hostname>.cc.0 (derived at startup, not hardcoded); a second CC launch on the same host gets <hostname>.cc.1; tmux ls shows the new names; Granny dispatches to the correct session; CC_TMUX_SESSION env var still overrides.
